title: Wire extra-packages.yaml as Docker build source of truth
summary: Review of changes addressing external review #3 from greptile-apps[bot]. Two changes were made: (1) Removed mandatory non-empty guards (::error:: + exit 1) for pip_extras and apt_packages in the CI workflow's 'Resolve extra packages' step, making all three fields (pip_extras, pip_packages, apt_packages) uniformly tolerant of empty values. This is correct — the Dockerfile already handles empty values via conditional guards ([ -n "$PIP_EXTRAS" ] and if [ -n "${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES}" ]). (2) Removed unnecessary cp -a / rm -rf of the Keystone source tree in the Dockerfile, using the bind-mounted /tmp/keystone directly for uv pip install. Both changes are sound. However, docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md and docs/reference/container-images.md were not updated to reflect these changes, leaving stale descriptions of removed behavior.

tests_checklist.item: test_extra_packages_valid_yaml_structure validates pip_extras, pip_packages, apt_packages fields
test_extra_packages_build_wiring confirms Dockerfile ARGs and workflow wiring
test_no_hardcoded_apt_packages confirms no hardcoded package names in Dockerfile

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing yq + $GITHUB_OUTPUT pattern from 'Resolve source ref' step
All three fields handled uniformly (no asymmetric validation)
Dockerfile conditional guards correctly handle empty ARG values

security_checklist.item: yq input from checked-in YAML file (not user-supplied)
EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES expansion in apt-get install is safe (values from controlled YAML)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal — only removes over-strict validation
Dockerfile handles empty values defensively without CI-level guards
Consistent empty-value handling across pipeline stages

fail_fast_checklist.item: yq // [] fallback prevents null errors from missing fields
Dockerfile guards prevent apt-get failure on empty package list

defensive_checklist.item: Dockerfile [ -n "$PIP_EXTRAS" ] guard prevents empty extras bracket
Dockerfile if [ -n "${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES}" ] guard prevents apt-get error
PIP_PACKAGES trailing space on empty is harmless to uv pip install

issues_found.description: Stale documentation: step 6 description still says 'Fails with ::error:: if pip_extras or apt_packages are empty (CC-0027)' but this behavior was removed in the workflow. The CI step now allows empty values for all three fields.
issues_found.fix: Update the step 6 description to: 'Reads releases/<release>/extra-packages.yaml via yq to extract pip_extras (comma-joined), pip_packages (space-joined), and apt_packages (space-joined). Empty values are passed through — the Dockerfile handles them via conditional guards (CC-0027).'

issues_found.description: Stale Dockerfile code example in the 'Named Build Contexts' section still shows the old cp -a / rm -rf pattern (cp -a /tmp/keystone /tmp/keystone-build, rm -rf /tmp/keystone-build) that was removed from the actual Dockerfile. The code now uses the bind-mounted /tmp/keystone directly.
issues_found.fix:
Update the code block at lines 177-185 to match the current Dockerfile:
```dockerfile
ARG PIP_EXTRAS=""
ARG PIP_PACKAGES=""

RUN --mount=type=bind,from=upper-constraints,source=upper-constraints.txt,target=/tmp/upper-constraints.txt \
    --mount=type=bind,from=keystone,target=/tmp/keystone \
    PKG="/tmp/keystone" && \
    [ -n "$PIP_EXTRAS" ] && PKG="${PKG}[${PIP_EXTRAS}]" || true && \
    uv pip install --prefix /var/lib/openstack \
        --constraint /tmp/upper-constraints.txt \
        "$PKG" $PIP_PACKAGES
```

next_steps: Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md:154 to remove the stale 'Fails with ::error::' description
Update docs/reference/container-images.md:177-185 to show current Dockerfile code without cp -a / rm -rf
